Title: Combining multiparametric MRI features-based transfer learning and clinical parameters: application of machine learning for the differentiation of uterine sarcomas from atypical leiomyomas.
AI_MAYBE_Reason: Explicit transfer learning (ResNet50, ImageNet transfer, low-tier FM) vs radiomics comparison on same uterine sarcoma vs ALM cohort with separate AUCs across T2/DWI/T2-DWI/complex models.
Reason_Quote_From_Paper: he study meets all inclusion criteria by directly comparing ImageNet-supervised CNN features (low-tier foundation models) with PyRadiomics-derived texture features (GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, NGTDM, GLDM) on a shared internal test cohort of human patients undergoing multiparametric MRI scans, while reporting distinct, separate performance metrics for both methodologies.
FM_Named: "Four pretrained 2D CNNs (InceptionV3, Resnet50, Inception Resnet V2, Xception) were used to extract deep-learning features from T2WI and DWI images."
Texture_Features: PyRadiomics3.0 version was used to extract radiomics features of the ROI on MRI images. A total of 1288 radiomics features included the following: (1) 14 shape features, (2) 252 first-order histogram features, and (3) texture features: 308 grey level cooccurrence matrix (GLCM), 224 grey level run length matrix (GLRLM), 224 grey level size zone matrix (GLSZM), 70 neighbouring grey tone difference matrix (NGTDM), and 196 grey level dependence matrix (GLDM)
Per_Arm_Metrics: In the test set, the T2, DWI, and T2-DWI models based on transfer learning (AUCs range from 0.76 to 0.81, 0.80 to 0.88, and 0.85 to 0.92, respectively) outperformed the models based on radiomics (AUCs of 0.73, 0.76, and 0.79, respectively).
Modality_OK: Objectives To explore the feasibility and effectiveness of machine learning (ML) based on multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging (mp-MRI) features extracted from transfer learning combined with clinical parameters to differentiate uterine sarcomas from atypical leiomyomas (ALMs).
Notes: NA
